verses.text: He dwells among a people with eyes that see not and ears that hear not; he is to pack his belongings and move in their sight. Acted warning. God dramatizes the coming exile for those too dull to hear words.

He dwells among a people with eyes that see not and ears that hear not; he is to pack his belongings and move in their sight. Acted warning. God dramatizes the coming exile for those too dull to hear words.

He dwells among a people with eyes that see not and ears that hear not; he is to pack his belongings and move in their sight. Acted warning. God dramatizes the coming exile for those too dull to hear words.
He carries out his goods as for captivity, digs through the wall, and covers his face — a sign to Israel. Vivid prophecy. The picture of flight is meant to pierce a blind heart.
He carries out his goods as for captivity, digs through the wall, and covers his face — a sign to Israel. Vivid prophecy. The picture of flight is meant to pierce a blind heart.
He carries out his goods as for captivity, digs through the wall, and covers his face — a sign to Israel. Vivid prophecy. The picture of flight is meant to pierce a blind heart.
He does as commanded, and in the morning the people ask, 'What doest thou?' Curiosity at last. The strange sign opens an ear that plain speech could not.
He does as commanded, and in the morning the people ask, 'What doest thou?' Curiosity at last. The strange sign opens an ear that plain speech could not.
He does as commanded, and in the morning the people ask, 'What doest thou?' Curiosity at last. The strange sign opens an ear that plain speech could not.
He explains: this concerns 'the prince in Jerusalem,' who shall shoulder his goods and go forth in the twilight. The king's flight foretold. None, however high, escapes the judgment of God.
He explains: this concerns 'the prince in Jerusalem,' who shall shoulder his goods and go forth in the twilight. The king's flight foretold. None, however high, escapes the judgment of God.
He explains: this concerns 'the prince in Jerusalem,' who shall shoulder his goods and go forth in the twilight. The king's flight foretold. None, however high, escapes the judgment of God.
God will spread His net on him; he shall be brought to Babylon yet 'not see it,' though he die there. Zedekiah, blinded, fulfilled it. God's word comes true even in its strangest details.
God will spread His net on him; he shall be brought to Babylon yet 'not see it,' though he die there. Zedekiah, blinded, fulfilled it. God's word comes true even in its strangest details.
They shall know the LORD when scattered, yet He will leave a few to confess their abominations among the nations. A purpose in the remnant. The survivors become witnesses to God's justice.
They shall know the LORD when scattered, yet He will leave a few to confess their abominations among the nations. A purpose in the remnant. The survivors become witnesses to God's justice.
He is to eat his bread with quaking and drink with trembling, signifying the land's coming desolation 'for the violence' of its people. Fear acted out. Sin's harvest is dread and emptiness.

He silences the proverb, 'The days are prolonged, and every vision faileth'; the days are at hand, and 'the word which I shall speak shall come to pass.' Delay breeds scoffing. But God's word, however long deferred, is sure.

He silences the proverb, 'The days are prolonged, and every vision faileth'; the days are at hand, and 'the word which I shall speak shall come to pass.' Delay breeds scoffing. But God's word, however long deferred, is sure.
Others say his visions are for far-off times; God replies that none of His words shall be delayed any longer. Procrastination undone. What God speaks, He will swiftly perform.
